summary: Round 9 reopens HNSW from the round-8 hard stop rather than from a success state. The graph-quality line closed with a real authority-lane regression for Rust HNSW, so the next explicit hypothesis is search_fastpath_cleanup: remove profiling-oriented overhead from the production layer-0 L2 hot path and cache batch-4 dispatch before deciding whether any lightweight search-side code-quality gain still exists.
action: Keep the historical HNSW family verdict unchanged, freeze the round-8 hard-stop evidence as the round-9 baseline, and require a fresh search-fastpath audit plus another same-schema authority rerun before reopening any broader HNSW performance narrative.
